g.data-narration: A data structure is a specialized format for organizing, processing, and storing data that enables efficient access and modification. It provides a systematic way to manage and organize information for computational use.
g.text: A specialized format for organizing, processing, and storing data that enables
efficient access and modification. It manages large amounts of data effectively.

g.data-narration: An Abstract Data Type defines a data type by its behavior through operations, independent of implementation details. It specifies what operations can be performed without revealing how they are implemented in code.
g.text: A mathematical model that defines a data type by its behavior (operations)
independent of implementation. Examples: List, Stack, Queue, Dictionary.

g.data-narration: Data structures are classified as primitive (int, float, char, pointer) or non-primitive (linear and non-linear structures). Primitive types store simple values while non-primitive types organize complex data relationships efficiently.
g.text: Primitive: Basic types (int, float, char, pointer) | Non-Primitive: User-defined
Linear: Array, Linked List, Stack, Queue | Non-Linear: Trees, Graphs

g.data-narration: Time complexity measures execution time using Big-O notation: O(1) constant is fastest, followed by O(log n) logarithmic, O(n) linear, O(n log n) linearithmic, and O(n²) quadratic which is slowest.
g.text: Time Complexity: O(1) Constant < O(log n) Logarithmic < O(n) Linear < O(n log n)
Linearithmic < O(n²) Quadratic. Space Complexity: Memory required as function of input size.

g.data-narration: Fundamental operations on data structures include traversal to visit all elements, insertion to add new elements, deletion to remove elements, search to find specific values, sorting to arrange elements in order, and merging to combine structures.
g.text: Traversal: Access each element | Insertion: Add new elements | Deletion: Remove elements
Search: Find specific element | Sorting: Arrange in order | Merging: Combine two structures

g.data-narration: Choosing the right data structure determines algorithm efficiency and can make applications run in milliseconds instead of minutes. Proper selection of data structures is crucial for building fast and scalable software systems.
